sectionsArray.headline: A starter, not a one-off
sectionsArray.subheadline: This project encodes content architecture decisions once — schema factories, fetch patterns, cache tags, live preview, and agent-ready docs — so every Sanity + Next.js site starts from a known baseline.

sectionsArray.headline: What we optimize for
sectionsArray.subheadline: Principles that keep content models clean and frontends predictable.

features.title: Content-first routing
features.description: Pages own URIs. Articles live under a fixed namespace. Editors control URLs without touching code.

features.title: Schema as system
features.description: Reusable field factories for links, media, SEO, and page builder sections — not copy-paste schema.

features.title: Closed page builder
features.description: Curated sections only — no arbitrary HTML. Marketing pages compose; articles use Portable Text.

features.title: Docs as source of truth
features.description: AGENTS.md and the guide site encode decisions so teams and AI assistants extend the starter consistently.

sectionsArray.headline: Ready to set it up?
sectionsArray.subheadline: Clone, configure env vars, import seed content, and deploy — step by step in the documentation.

seoMetadata.title: About
seoMetadata.description: About the ACTTA Studio Sanity + Next.js starter.

sectionsArray.headline: ACTTA Studio Sanity Starter
sectionsArray.subheadline: A production foundation for content-driven marketing sites — embedded Studio, URI-based pages, live preview, cache tags, and a page builder built on content architecture principles.

sectionsArray.headline: Built for production
sectionsArray.subheadline: Everything you need to ship a Sanity-powered site — not a tutorial project.

features.title: Content architecture
features.description: Clear document roles — pages, articles, site settings — with schema factories and a curated Studio surface.

features.title: Page builder
features.description: Hero, features, steps, CTA, text banner, and contact form sections. Articles use Portable Text instead.

features.title: Live preview
features.description: Presentation Tool with in-place section updates and live site header and footer in draft mode.

features.title: Cache & webhooks
features.description: Tag-based revalidation on publish. Draft preview works without webhooks.

features.title: SEO & forms
features.description: Per-route metadata, sitemap, CMS favicon, contact submissions, and optional Umami tracking.

features.title: Full documentation
features.description: Setup, commands, feature guides, and architecture notes on a dedicated documentation site.

sectionsArray.headline: This site demonstrates the starter
sectionsArray.subheadline: Pages and articles you see here are seed content. Setup, env vars, deployment, and webhooks are documented separately.

sectionsArray.headline: Start building
sectionsArray.subheadline: Follow the getting-started guide to clone, configure, and deploy your own instance.

seoMetadata.title: ACTTA Studio Sanity Starter
seoMetadata.description: Production-ready Sanity + Next.js starter for content-driven marketing sites.

title: Content architecture
description: Principles behind the starter's content model and Studio surface.

title: What is content architecture?
excerpt: Why CMS structure matters before schema — and how this starter applies those ideas.

children.text: Content architecture is how you organize a CMS so editors know what they are responsible for and the frontend knows what to expect. It is not page design — it is document roles, route ownership, and Studio navigation.
Schema mistakes are fixable. Surface mistakes compound. If the Studio is confusing on day one, that confusion leaks into routing, queries, and components.
In this starter
Pages own URIs. Articles own slugs under /articles/. Site holds global config. The page builder is a closed set of sections. Those boundaries are documented in AGENTS.md and on the guide site.
For the full principles and checklists, see the content architecture guide at sanity-starter-guide.vercel.app.

seoMetadata.title: What is content architecture?
seoMetadata.description: Introduction to content architecture in the ACTTA Studio Sanity starter.

title: Document roles in this starter
excerpt: Each document type has one job — homepage, page, article, site, and submissions.

children.text: Every document type in this starter has a bounded responsibility. Mixing roles creates fragile frontends and confused editors.
Route-bound content
Homepage — singleton page at /, not creatable or deletable
Page — owns a full URI such as /about or /contact; composed with the page builder
Article — slug only; the app serves /articles/[slug]
Global and generated
Site — nav, footer, SEO defaults, redirects, 404 page, form emails, auth toggle
Contact form submission — read-only records created by the server action

seoMetadata.title: Document roles in this starter
seoMetadata.description: Page, article, site, and submission document roles in ACTTA Studio.

title: Why articles use a fixed namespace
excerpt: Editors set the slug — the application owns the /articles/ prefix.

children.text: Articles always live under /articles/[slug]. Editors never set the full path — only the slug segment. Pages, by contrast, own their complete URI.
Why it matters
A popular post keeps a stable URL prefix even if you reorganize marketing pages. Search engines and shared links stay valid when an editor renames a slug — the namespace does not drift.
Categories classify articles but do not get public routes by default. Use them in list views or filters as your product needs.

seoMetadata.title: Why articles use a fixed namespace
seoMetadata.description: Fixed /articles/ namespace for editorial content in ACTTA Studio.

title: The page builder as a closed system
excerpt: Curated sections instead of arbitrary blocks — and why articles use Portable Text.

children.text: Marketing pages compose from a closed set of sections: hero, features, steps, CTA, text banner, and contact form. There is no generic HTML block or embed escape hatch.
Pages vs articles
Pages use the page builder for layout. Articles use Portable Text for long-form body content — excerpt, cover image, and categories — because editorial posts are not landing-page compositions.
Extending
New sections are scaffolded with pnpm plop — schema, React component, and registry in one step. See the page builder guide on the documentation site for the full section reference.

seoMetadata.title: The page builder as a closed system
seoMetadata.description: Closed page builder and Portable Text articles in ACTTA Studio.

title: Surface before schema
excerpt: Editors experience navigation and labels — design the Studio as a product first.

children.text: Editors do not experience your file structure. They experience the Studio sidebar, field groups, previews, and labels. That surface becomes their mental model of the site.
This starter's sidebar
Homepage — singleton, always at /
Pages, articles, categories — route-bound or editorial content
Form submissions — read-only incoming data
Site — global config that affects the whole property
Every document type added to the sidebar is cognitive tax. Add types deliberately — and read the Studio structure guide for how this maps to code.

seoMetadata.title: Surface before schema
seoMetadata.description: Designing the Sanity Studio surface before schema in ACTTA Studio.
